# worldbench

> One prompt, every model, one floating island each. worldbench is a
> human-as-judge benchmark: a single natural-language prompt asks each model to
> generate a self-contained Three.js scene of a floating, multi-biome voxel
> island — from text alone, with no image, no schema, and no reference file —
> and the resulting worlds are shown side by side so a person can judge for
> themselves which model's world actually holds together.

There is no rubric and no automated scorer. Every model receives the exact same
prompt and must produce a single `world.html` that opens in a browser and runs.
Getting it right is more than scene styling; the prompt encodes a placement
graph and an ecology, so the model has to reason about several things at once:

- **Spatial / relational placement** — biomes must sit in the right adjacency
  (snow mountains behind the conifer forest, beside the highlands, sloping into
  a flowering grove), not just be scattered.
- **Ecological causality** — water behaves like water: mountain melt feeds a wet
  corridor, the desert is a rain shadow, lava touching water turns to obsidian.
- **Elevation and scale** — peaks dominate, plains stay low, each biome reads at
  its own scale from a high orbit.
- **Ecological grounding of flora and fauna** — cacti in the desert, pines in
  the snow forest, deer at jungle edges.
- **Temporal / systems reasoning** — day/night, seasons, and cyclic weather
  staggered by biome.
- **UI and interaction logic** — a clickable biome legend and a free orbit
  camera baked into the single file.

The site is front-end only: browse the worlds, open each one live in an iframe,
and compare how different models interpreted the same instruction.

## The prompt

- [prompt.md](/prompt.md): the exact, only model-facing prompt — copy and paste
  it as-is into any model to reproduce a run.

## Pages

- [Home](/): floating-island hero.
- [About](/about): what worldbench is and the kind of reasoning it tests.
- [All tests](/tests): grid of every model's world.

## Tests

Each links to a detail page with intro media and the live `world.html` embedded
in an iframe; the raw output is served at `/tests/<slug>/world.html`.

- [Claude Fable 5 (Anthropic)](/tests/fable)
- [GPT-5.6 Sol (OpenAI)](/tests/gpt-5-6-sol)
- [Gemini 3.1 Pro (Google)](/tests/gemini-3-1-pro)
- [Grok 4.5 Fast (xAI)](/tests/grok-4-5-fast)
- [Qwen 3.8 Max Preview (Alibaba)](/tests/qwen-3-8-max-prev)
- [Kimi K2.7 (Moonshot AI)](/tests/kimi-k-2-7)
- [Kimi K3 (Moonshot AI)](/tests/kimi-k-3)
- [GLM 5 Turbo (Z.ai)](/tests/glm-5-turbo)
